description: Normal-road lateral-crossing boundary test: a truck enters from the left and is deterministically placed to intersect the ego path at the swept TTC; the nominal right-side escape corridor is both blocked and occupied by a pedestrian, while a follower behind the ego makes hard braking potentially costly. The grid concentrates on the short/actionable TTC band below and just touching existing blocked-escape rules.
hypothesis: When the right escape side is blocked by a pedestrian/corridor constraint, braking should dominate at longer TTC or larger follower gap, but close follower plus high ego speed/high truck lateral speed should shift dominance toward hold-course or left/behind-threat drift rather than right T-drift.
